sentence_reviews.text: k-nearest-neighbor methods predict from nearby stored examples, while Naive Bayes applies Bayes’ rule under class-conditional feature assumptions.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA, Ch. 19 nonparametric models including nearest-neighbor models and probabilistic learning; PML nearest neighbors and naive Bayes / Bayesian classifiers topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: The definition is standard and supported across the cited textbook topics, though it combines two methods in one sentence.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of k-NN & Naive Bayes is to connect Datasets, sampling & splits, Embeddings & distributed representations to downstream capabilities including Recommendation, ranking & personalization, Tabular predictive analytics & decision support.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA nearest-neighbor/nonparametric learning and probabilistic learning; PML data, nearest-neighbor, naive Bayes, and supervised learning topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: The data and supervised-learning connections are supported, but embeddings and downstream recommendation/tabular decision-support claims are broad graph synthesis.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML nearest-neighbor/supervised learning topics; no direct recommender-system evidence in packet sources.
sentence_reviews.notes: Nearest-neighbor ideas are plausible in recommendation, but the packet citations do not directly establish the downstream substrate claim.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Tabular predictive analytics & decision support
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA and PML supervised classification/regression topics, including nearest-neighbor and naive Bayes classifiers.
sentence_reviews.notes: Both methods are standard supervised classifiers for feature-table data; 'decision support' remains atlas phrasing.

sentence_reviews.text: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Datasets, sampling & splits, Embeddings & distributed representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA nearest-neighbor search/nonparametric models and probabilistic learning; PML model selection and classifier topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited sources support model families and data dependence, but the limitation wording and embeddings dependency are only partially evidenced.

sentence_reviews.text: k-NN & Naive Bayes consumes, emits, or organizes information using Datasets, sampling & splits.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA learning from examples and nearest-neighbor methods; PML data and classifier topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Both methods operate over labeled examples/data; sampling/splits are broader evaluation context.

sentence_reviews.text: k-NN & Naive Bayes consumes, emits, or organizes information using Embeddings & distributed representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA nearest-neighbor search and representation topics; PML representation/feature and nearest-neighbor topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: k-NN can operate on embedded representations, but naive Bayes does not require embeddings and the packet sources do not establish this as common for the combined node.

sentence_reviews.text: k-NN & Naive Bayes is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Bayesian & approximate inference.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA probabilistic learning / naive Bayes; PML naive Bayes and Bayesian classification topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Naive Bayes uses Bayes' rule and probabilistic modeling, but k-NN is not Bayesian inference; the combined-node edge is only partially supported.

sentence_reviews.text: k-NN & Naive Bayes follows the learning setup expressed by Supervised learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA learning from examples and classifier topics; PML supervised classification with kNN/naive Bayes.
sentence_reviews.notes: Both are standard supervised classification methods.

edge_reviews.notes: Both methods depend on example datasets; represents_with/core is defensible for this atlas relation.
Embedding representations can be used with k-NN, but this is not established for naive Bayes or as a common dependency by the packet evidence.
The Bayesian connection is direct for naive Bayes but not k-NN; relationship type is partially supported, common strength is weak for the combined node.
Both methods are standard supervised predictors/classifiers; trained_under/common is supported.
